findings.description: Hook ownership validation incomplete in report_done() — allowed hooks to report_done for unowned tasks when owner_actor was empty.
Plan/epic closure used raw DB connections without retry wrapper — vulnerable to SQLITE_BUSY under contention.
v3 migration backfills state only when state='open'. Low likelihood edge case — acceptable.
verify_and_close() is not idempotent (raises on already-closed). By design.
save_baseline() uses direct write instead of atomic temp+rename. Acceptable for single-threaded CLI.

principles.name: Surgical Changes
principles.notes: Each plan touches only listed files

principles.name: Prefer shared utility packages over hand-rolled helpers
Validate boundaries
principles.notes: Role enforcement at API boundary, hook ownership validated

security.inputValidation: pass — _ALLOWED_FIELDS whitelist, actor_role enum, state machine enforcement
security.authorization: pass — role enforcement on close/report_done/verify_and_close, hook ownership fixed
security.secrets: pass — no credentials in code or artifacts

evidence: Operating principles checked: Think Before Coding, Simplicity First, Surgical Changes, Goal-Driven Execution, Prefer shared utility packages over hand-rolled helpers, Don't probe data YOLO-style, Validate boundaries, Typed SDKs
Automated checks: lint: pass, types: skipped, tests: skipped, workflow_validate: pass
